channel.title: Data Visualisation on Data Management Training
channel.description: Recent content in Data Visualisation on Data Management Training

item.title: Introduction to Data Visualization
item.description:
TODO: Add the YouTube embed for this video when the link is available.

This video introduces the concept of data visualization, why it is important, and the principles of effective data visualization.

item.title: Good Practices for Data Visualization
item.description:
TODO: Add the YouTube embed for this video when the link is available.

This video covers chart types, choosing the right chart, simplifying charts, using labels and titles well, selecting colour schemes, and avoiding common mistakes such as 3D charts.

item.title: Data Visualisation Assessment
item.description:
This assessment is optional but highly recommended. Javascript-based answer checking will be added later.

  1. What is the primary purpose of data visualisation? a. To add more complexity to data X. To simplify complex data for easier understanding c. To hide important information d. To make data confusing

  2. Which of the below indicate the importance of data visualisation? (Multiple choice) a. It enables us to see patterns and trends that might not be apparent from looking at the raw data b. It makes data analysis more complicated and time consuming X. It enables us to make data-driven decisions based on facts rather than opinions or assumptions d. It is only useful for experts in data analysis

item.title: Data Visualisation Assignment
item.description:
1. Go to https://dashboard.udiseplus.gov.in/#/reportDashboard/sReport
  2. Find and click on the report “Number of Schools by Availability of Infrastructure and Facilities, School Management and School Category” (report ID: 3013)
  3. Select latest year, West Bengal state, and District-wise in the district option
  4. Download the data by clicking on the green excel icon on the top right of the report card
  5. After downloading the file, open it in MS Excel.
  6. Add a filter to the data, as you may have learnt in Module 1.
  7. Filter the Location column and get the data for your district only.
  8. Filter further the School Management column to get information on Department of Education schools.
  9. Select this entire data and paste into a new sheet, call it TABLE 1. This is all infrastructure related information for your district and schools under SED.
  10. Add a sum formula at the end of your TABLE 1 and find the total number of schools in each column.
  11. Find how many schools have a Playground in total and how many do not have a playground and create a separate summary table for this. Call it TABLE 2.
  12. TABLE 2 should look like the exhibit below:
